author: jsign
content:
We rely on the same state-tracking of BALs for execution witness creation, so both should be coherent.
This was an initial decision we did to minimize complexity and just leverage that BALs impose a strict state accessing that wasn't previously observable.

Ofc, as you know from the other CALL-* convo, BALs might not be minimal in some border cases thus the same will happen with witnesses. The main reason I proposed the famous "Option 2" was to make it minimal for the witness (and thus BALs) :) (But I think the conclusion was to go with Option 1 which is not be optimal)

TL;DR: Yes, witness will be coherent with BALs since in EEST we rely on the same logic of state tracker or when code is accessed to capture information in the execution witness.

author: chfast
content: I get it, but also not very happy that we give up on relatively easy fixes now which will become hard to fix later.

author: jsign
content:
I can't agree more with you. But this is more like a BAL discussion than an executionWitness one.
I definitely don't want EL clients to have specia lrules for executionWitness different from BALs -- it is buying a lot of complexity that I don't people to maintain.

If EL teams decide to give up on relatively easy fixes to minimize BALs, they are indirectly also affecting the execution witness (at least for those border cases). But I put first not making things more complex to "workaround" this situation.
